articles.title: Intel-AMD veterans' Agrani Labs scouts for $100 million to build AI chip
articles.description: Agrani Labs, founded by former Intel and AMD executives, is aiming for a significant $100 million Series A funding. This investment will support the development of AI inference chips designed to work with Nvidia's CUDA software. The company is in talks with Qualcomm and Battery Ventures for potential investment.

articles.description: The Moto G37 and Moto G37 Power were unveiled in global markets last month, and the two smartphones are now headed to India. Motorola India has confirmed that the Moto G37 and G37 Power will launch in the country on May 19. Both smartphones will be available through Flipkart, Motorola’s official online store, and select offline retail outlets. The Moto G37 will feature a 6.7-inch display with a 120Hz refresh rate, a dual rear camera setup led by a 50MP...

articles.title: Google may cut free storage to 5GB but there is a way users can get full 15GB
articles.description: Google appears to be testing a new policy that reduces free cloud storage for new accounts to 5GB. Users can unlock the full 15GB by linking a phone number, a move Google states is to ensure one pool per person and prevent bots. Existing accounts are unlikely to be affected by this change.

articles.title: Anthropic to donate $200 million to Bill Gates' Gates Foundation
articles.description: Anthropic and the Gates Foundation are committing $200 million over four years to advance AI for public good, focusing on global health, life sciences, education, and economic mobility. This partnership aims to accelerate vaccine development, improve health data utilization, and enhance educational tools for K-12 students and adult learners worldwide.

articles.title: Mass layoffs at Goldman Sachs? President John Waldron says "digital agents will be our robots"
articles.description: Goldman Sachs is integrating generative AI 'robots' to automate tasks, aiming to boost productivity and cut costs without mass layoffs. President John Waldron stated these digital agents will enhance the firm's 'human assembly line,' creating new tech roles. He clarified recent job cuts are unrelated to AI, attributing them to post-COVID adjustments and evolving needs.

articles.title: Who is Nitrogen group, what does Foxconn do and what data was hacked? Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn
articles.description: Who is Nitrogen group, what does Foxconn do and what data was hacked? Foxconn confirmed a cyberattack that affected some North American factories. The ransomware group Nitrogen claimed the breach and said it stole more than 11 million files and eight terabytes of data. The files may include customer information, product schematics, and bank statements. Foxconn said operations are resuming while cybersecurity teams continue response work and investigation.
